name: Brandon Grotesque
classification_notes: Brandon Grotesque sits between geometric and grotesque traditions, combining the clean circular forms of 1920s geometric sans-serifs with the warmth and slight irregularity of hand-drawn grotesques.
summary: A geometric grotesque by Hannes von Döhren that blends circular letterforms with a slightly humanist warmth, widely used in branding and editorial work since 2010.
description: Designed by Hannes von Döhren and released through HvD Fonts in 2010, Brandon Grotesque draws on the geometric sans traditions of the 1920s while softening the rigidity with subtle irregularities in stroke weight and spacing. The family spans six weights from Thin to Black with matching italics, and extends into Brandon Grotesque Narrow and the more text-optimised Brandon Text. Its readability and clean character have made it a staple of contemporary branding and print design.
foundry_blurb: HvD Fonts present Brandon Grotesque as a geometric sans with roots in the geometric typefaces of the 1920s and 30s, designed by Hannes von Döhren. The family is noted for its legibility and versatility across weights and applications, from display sizes through to body text.

name: Niveau Serif
classification_notes: Niveau Serif is a humanist serif designed to complement Niveau Grotesk, sharing its warm proportions and optical balance. Its serifs are low-contrast with a contemporary feel, placing it in the humanist serif category.
summary: A humanist serif by Hannes von Döhren designed as a companion to Niveau Grotesk, sharing its warm proportions and structured rhythm.
description: Designed by Hannes von Döhren at HvD Fonts, Niveau Serif pairs directly with Niveau Grotesk through shared optical sizing, proportions and overall texture. Low-contrast serifs and open apertures give it readability at text sizes while retaining enough character for display use. A practical choice for editorial and corporate projects that need a serif and sans-serif from the same visual family.
foundry_blurb: HvD Fonts present Niveau Serif as the serif companion to Niveau Grotesk, designed by Hannes von Döhren. The family is built for versatility across text and display settings, with a range of weights and italics to suit varied typographic needs.

description: Fabrikat Mono extends the Fabrikat family into a fixed-width format, carrying the geometric clarity of the parent design into contexts that demand monospaced spacing. name: Brix Slab
classification_notes: Brix Slab is a slab serif companion to the Brix Sans family, sharing its humanist proportions and stroke modulation with bracketed slab serifs appended.
summary: A humanist slab serif from HvD Fonts designed as a companion to the Brix Sans family, suited to text and editorial work.
description: Sharing the humanist proportions and warmth of its sans counterpart, Brix Slab adds bracketed slab serifs to produce a legible text face with personality. The range of weights from Thin to Black, each with matching italics, gives it flexibility across print and digital editorial contexts. Its even colour and moderate contrast make it a reliable choice for body copy as well as display use.
foundry_blurb: HvD Fonts present Brix Slab as a slab serif companion to Brix Sans, designed by Hannes von Döhren. The family spans a wide weight range with italics throughout, intended for versatile use in text and display settings.

description: Brix Slab Condensed extends the Brix Slab family into a tighter width, making it suitable for compact column widths, newspaper-style layouts and headline work where a slab serif presence is needed without the space demand.
